title: podman run fails with run/systemd/journal/socket: sendmsg: permission denied on Ubuntu 24.04
body:
### Issue Description

After podman installation on Ubuntu 24.4, running a container causes the following issue:
```
$ podman run -it busybox sh --debug
ERRO[0000] Unable to write container event: write unixgram @3f65b->/run/systemd/journal/socket: sendmsg: permission denied 
WARN[0000] Cannot open current network namespace /proc/106344/task/106361/ns/net: "open /proc/106344/task/106361/ns/net: permission denied" 
ERRO[0000] Unable to write image event: "write unixgram @3f65b->/run/systemd/journal/socket: sendmsg: permission denied" 
Error: creating network namespace for container f95daa4d7f389d14710c1654c277093fe8167b173d3c775dc2093dffa0cf43aa: failed to create namespace: open /proc/106344/task/106361/ns/net: permission denied

```

### Steps to reproduce the issue

Steps to reproduce the issue
```bash
1. sudo apt install podman podman-docker
2. podman --version # gives 4.9.3
3. sudo usermod --add-subuids 10000-300000 --add-subgids 10000-300000 $USER && sudo podman system migrate # needed for some images
4. podman run -it busybox sh```

### Describe the results you received

Error displayed on container not running:
```
ERRO[0000] Unable to write container event: write unixgram @3f65b->/run/systemd/journal/socket: sendmsg: permission denied 
WARN[0000] Cannot open current network namespace /proc/106344/task/106361/ns/net: "open /proc/106344/task/106361/ns/net: permission denied" 
ERRO[0000] Unable to write image event: "write unixgram @3f65b->/run/systemd/journal/socket: sendmsg: permission denied" 
Error: creating network namespace for container f95daa4d7f389d14710c1654c277093fe8167b173d3c775dc2093dffa0cf43aa: failed to create namespace: open /proc/106344/task/106361/ns/net: permission denied
```

### Describe the results you expected

Container running

### Podman in a container

No

### Privileged Or Rootless

Rootless

### Upstream Latest Release

No

$ podman --version && cat /etc/os-release && uname -a

### Additional information

It was working fine of Ubuntu 22.04

comments.body: Can you reproduce with 5.4.0 from upstream? We only provide support for the latest version upstream.

Thanks @mheon , indeed is it working with the latest 5.4.0 version, which is not the one installed by apt on Ubuntu 24.04.

For the ones with the same problem, 

1. Uninstall podman with apt: `sudo apt remove podman --purge && sudo apt autoremove`
2. Install podman with homebrew: 
```bash
# If you don' have brew, check from https://brew.sh/
brew install podman
```

And it is fixed :-) Thanks!
